meta.scene_summary: We're busy building the future, but we keep forgetting to lock the front door.

cooking.headline: Rocket Lab is buying Iridium to build the ultimate space-based group project
cooking.summary: Rocket Lab just decided that launching rockets is too lonely and bought a whole satellite constellation to keep them company. It's the orbital equivalent of buying a house just to make sure your neighbors can't complain about your late-night coding sessions.
cooking.plain_english: Rocket Lab has acquired the satellite communications company Iridium to integrate satellite hardware with its own rocket launch services.

cooking.headline: Central bankers are worried the AI bubble is about to pop
cooking.summary: Central bankers are warning that the massive bet on AI could trigger a global financial crash if the hype doesn't translate to actual profits. It's like watching everyone go all-in on a startup idea during a hackathon without checking if the code actually compiles.
cooking.plain_english: International financial regulators have issued a warning that excessive investment and speculation in artificial intelligence could cause significant instability in the global economy.

cooking.headline: The supercomputer leaderboard just reset, and the winner is... bigger.
cooking.summary: The TOP500 list has a new king, proving that the only way to win in high-performance computing is to keep throwing more hardware at the problem. It's essentially a global arms race where the prize is just the right to be the fastest calculator in the room for six months.
cooking.plain_english: A new supercomputer has been ranked as the fastest in the world at the International Supercomputing Conference.

cooking.headline: Meta is using your Instagram photos to train its glasses, because you're the unpaid intern now
cooking.summary: Meta decided that your vacation photos are essentially free training data for their new smart glasses. It's like a group project where your friend uses your work for their presentation and forgets to even put your name on the slide.
cooking.plain_english: Meta is incorporating user-uploaded photos from Instagram into promotional materials and training data for their smart glasses product.

cooking.headline: Being the world's best AI model has the shelf life of a carton of milk
cooking.summary: GLM 5.2 is currently topping the leaderboards, which is a great achievement that will surely last until the next model drops on Tuesday. It is nice to see the AI arms race is officially at the stage where 'the best' is just a temporary title you hold until the next update finishes compiling.
cooking.plain_english: A new artificial intelligence model called GLM 5.2 has outperformed existing models in recent technical benchmark tests.

cooked.headline: One million passports leaked because security is just a suggestion
cooked.summary: A million passports just hit the internet, which is a fantastic way to ensure everyone's identity is now effectively public domain. It's the digital equivalent of leaving your house keys in the front door lock and hoping nobody notices you're on vacation.
cooked.plain_english: A database containing the personal information of one million people from their passport documents has been exposed online due to a security vulnerability.

cooked.headline: The memory giants are being sued for exactly the price fixing you suspected
cooked.summary: Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron are facing a lawsuit for allegedly coordinating memory prices to keep them artificially high. It's the corporate equivalent of three classmates agreeing to charge the same price for their homework, only to get caught by the teacher.
cooked.plain_english: Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron have been sued in the United States for allegedly colluding to manipulate the prices of memory chips.

cooked.headline: Ford realizes 'AI' doesn't know how to fix a transmission
cooked.summary: Ford is rehiring veteran engineers after realizing that AI models are great at hallucinating code but terrible at actually building cars. Turns out, there is no substitute for the kind of domain knowledge that only comes from twenty years of debugging real-world problems.
cooked.plain_english: Ford is bringing back retired engineers to help solve technical issues that artificial intelligence tools were unable to resolve.

cooked.headline: EU wants to scan your private messages, because privacy is just a legacy bug
cooked.summary: The EU is trying to revive a regime that scans private messages, effectively treating every user's inbox like a public repo that needs a security audit. It's a bold strategy to secure the internet by ensuring nobody has a private conversation left to protect.
cooked.plain_english: European authorities are attempting to reintroduce legislation that would allow for the scanning of private digital communications to detect illegal content.

cooked.headline: Buying movies on PlayStation is just renting them with extra steps
cooked.summary: Sony is removing Studio Canal movies from user libraries without offering refunds, proving that 'purchasing' digital media is just a long-term loan with a surprise expiration date. It turns out the only real owner of your movie collection is the company that can delete it from your console whenever they feel like it.
cooked.plain_english: PlayStation users who bought specific movies will lose access to them and will not be reimbursed for the cost.
